wiki.summary: "Chop Suey!" is a song by the American heavy metal band System of a Down. It was released on August 13, 2001, as the lead single from their second studio album, "Toxicity" (2001). The song earned the band its first Grammy nomination in 2002 for Best Metal Performance at the 44th Annual Grammy Awards. "Chop Suey!" is widely regarded as the band’s signature song, and its music video has accumulated over one billion views on YouTube. The lyrics of the song have been described as surrealist. Read more on Last.fm.
wiki.content: "Chop Suey!" is a song by the American heavy metal band System of a Down. It was released on August 13, 2001, as the lead single from their second studio album, "Toxicity" (2001). The song earned the band its first Grammy nomination in 2002 for Best Metal Performance at the 44th Annual Grammy Awards. "Chop Suey!" is widely regarded as the band’s signature song, and its music video has accumulated over one billion views on YouTube. The lyrics of the song have been described as surrealist. Guitarist Daron Malakian has stated that the song deals with the perception of death and how individuals are judged differently depending on the circumstances of their death. He referenced the lyric “I cry when angels deserve to die” in relation to this idea. The midsection of the song includes the line “Father, into your hands I commend my spirit”, taken from the sayings of Jesus on the cross, which singer Serj Tankian selected after randomly opening a book from producer Rick Rubin’s collection during a period of limited inspiration. Musically, "Chop Suey!" is characterized by abrupt shifts in dynamics, including contrasting heavy verses and melodic choruses. Tankian’s vocal performance has been noted for its precise and forceful delivery. The title of the song does not appear in the lyrics. The song was initially titled either "Suicide", according to bassist Shavo Odadjian, or "Self-Righteous Suicide", according to producer Rick Rubin. The title was later changed due to concerns from Columbia Records. Odadjian has stated that "Chop Suey!" is a wordplay on "suicide", implying the word is “chopped” in half. On some early album pressings, an introductory studio count-in and studio dialogue can be heard before the track begins. Read more on Last.fm.
